titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell Cooperative Extension Teaching and Information Dept. recordings21-24-950
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2019-12-03 23:53:16 +0000.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell Cooperative Extension Teaching and Information Dept. recordings

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Cooperative Extension. Teaching and Information Department

did.abstract: 183 twelve and sixteen inch records of speeches, interviews, and informal talks by such figures in the field of agriculture and related professions.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: 183 twelve and sixteen inch records of speeches, interviews, and informal talks by such figures in the field of agriculture and related professions as Bristow Adams, Howard E. Babcock, Liberty Hyde Bailey, Cornelius Betton, Louis Bromfield, Thomas E. Dewey, Edward R. Eastman, Glen W. Herrick, Carl Ladd, Clyde Moore, William I. Myers, Edward O'Neal, E. Lawrence Palmer, James E. Rice, James B. Sumner, and Jared Van Wagenen, Jr.; recordings made by well-known personalities in other fields such as Hans Bethe talking on atomic energy (1945), Edward R. Murrow on V-J Day (1945), and Anthony Eden and Bidault on V-E Day; recordings of important Cornell programs such as the Thurston Memorial Celebration (1939), and the launching of the victory ship CORNELL VICTORY (1945); recordings of other events of regional interest in New York State such as the inaugural program radio station WENY (Elmira), and two recorded programs of the New York State Historical Plays Series; recordings concerning the Nature Study Program at Cornell; numerous agricultural and home economics technical extension programs; and a number of recorded performances of Cornell University choral and instrumental groups.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell Cooperative Extension Teaching and Information Dept. recordings, #21-24-950. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Nuclear energy.
Nature study.
Home economics extension work -- New York (State)

Agriculture -- New York (State)
controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.
